mapper.select: select e.emp_id as EMP_ID, e.dept_id as DEPT_ID, e.position_id as POSITION_ID, e.username as USERNAME, e.password as PASSWORD, e.name as NAME, e.email as EMAIL, e.phone as PHONE, e.status_code as STATUS_CODE, e.hire_date as HIRE_DATE, e.is_deleted as IS_DELETED, e.version as VERSION, e.created_at as CREATED_AT, e.created_by as CREATED_BY, e.updated_at as UPDATED_AT, e.updated_by as UPDATED_BY from employees e where e.emp_id = #{empId} and e.is_deleted = 'n'

select e.emp_id as EMP_ID, e.dept_id as DEPT_ID, e.position_id as POSITION_ID, e.username as USERNAME, e.password as PASSWORD, e.name as NAME, e.email as EMAIL, e.phone as PHONE, e.status_code as STATUS_CODE, e.hire_date as HIRE_DATE, e.is_deleted as IS_DELETED, e.version as VERSION, e.created_at as CREATED_AT, e.created_by as CREATED_BY, e.updated_at as UPDATED_AT, e.updated_by as UPDATED_BY from employees e where e.username = #{username} and e.is_deleted = 'n'

select e.emp_id as EMP_ID, e.dept_id as DEPT_ID, e.position_id as POSITION_ID, e.username as USERNAME, e.password as PASSWORD, e.name as NAME, e.email as EMAIL, e.phone as PHONE, e.status_code as STATUS_CODE, e.hire_date as HIRE_DATE, e.is_deleted as IS_DELETED, e.version as VERSION, e.created_at as CREATED_AT, e.created_by as CREATED_BY, e.updated_at as UPDATED_AT, e.updated_by as UPDATED_BY from employees e where e.username = #{username}

select e.emp_id as EMP_ID, e.dept_id as DEPT_ID, e.position_id as POSITION_ID, e.username as USERNAME, e.password as PASSWORD, e.name as NAME, e.email as EMAIL, e.phone as PHONE, e.status_code as STATUS_CODE, e.hire_date as HIRE_DATE, e.is_deleted as IS_DELETED, e.version as VERSION, e.created_at as CREATED_AT, e.created_by as CREATED_BY, e.updated_at as UPDATED_AT, e.updated_by as UPDATED_BY from employees e where e.is_deleted = 'n' order by e.emp_id asc

select e.emp_id as EMP_ID, e.dept_id as DEPT_ID, e.position_id as POSITION_ID, e.username as USERNAME, e.password as PASSWORD, e.name as NAME, e.email as EMAIL, e.phone as PHONE, e.status_code as STATUS_CODE, e.hire_date as HIRE_DATE, e.is_deleted as IS_DELETED, e.version as VERSION, e.created_at as CREATED_AT, e.created_by as CREATED_BY, e.updated_at as UPDATED_AT, e.updated_by as UPDATED_BY from employees e where e.dept_id = #{deptId} and e.is_deleted = 'n' order by e.emp_id asc
select r.role_name as ROLE_NAME from employee_roles er inner join roles r on er.role_id = r.role_id where er.emp_id = #{empId} and er.is_deleted = 'n' and r.is_deleted = 'n' order by r.role_id asc
mapper.insert: insert into employees ( dept_id, position_id, username, password, name, email, phone, status_code, hire_date, is_deleted, version, created_at, created_by ) values ( #{deptId}, #{positionId}, #{username}, #{password}, #{name}, #{email}, #{phone}, #{statusCode}, #{hireDate}, #{isDeleted}, #{version}, #{createdAt}, #{createdBy} )
insert into employee_roles ( emp_id, role_id, is_deleted, version, created_at, created_by ) values ( #{empId}, #{roleId}, 'n', 0, #{createdAt}, #{createdBy} )
mapper.update: update employees set dept_id = #{deptId}, position_id = #{positionId}, name = #{name}, email = #{email}, phone = #{phone}, status_code = #{statusCode}, hire_date = #{hireDate}, version = #{version} + 1, updated_at = #{updatedAt}, updated_by = #{updatedBy} where emp_id = #{empId} and version = #{version} and is_deleted = 'n'
